questions.text: 'People as Resource' is a way of referring to a country’s working people in terms of their existing:
questions.explanation: It emphasizes the human factor as an asset that contributes to the Gross National Product.

questions.text: When existing 'human resource' is further developed by becoming more educated and healthy, it is called:
questions.explanation: Investments in education and health turn people into human capital, adding to the country's productive power.

questions.text: Which of the following is an investment in human capital?
questions.explanation: Just like physical capital, education and health yield a return in the form of higher income and productivity.

questions.text: Human capital is superior to other resources like land and physical capital because:
options: It can use land and capital
questions.explanation: Land and capital cannot become useful on their own; they require human intervention.

questions.text: The quality of population depends upon:
questions.explanation: A healthy, educated, and skilled population is an asset to the economy.

questions.text: Activities like agriculture, forestry, and fishing fall under which sector?
questions.explanation: Primary sector involves extraction and production of natural resources.

questions.text: Manufacturing and construction belong to the:
questions.explanation: Secondary sector converts raw materials into finished goods.

questions.text: Trade, transport, communication, and banking are examples of:
questions.explanation: The tertiary sector provides services rather than producing tangible goods.

questions.text: Economic activities are also called:
questions.explanation: Economic activities include market activities (for pay) and non-market activities (for self-consumption).

questions.text: The literacy rate in India was highest in which state (as per 2011 census)?
questions.explanation: Kerala consistently has the highest literacy rates in India due to high social investment.

questions.text: Which scheme was launched to provide elementary education to all children in the age group of 6-14 years?
questions.explanation: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan is a significant step towards the goal of universal elementary education.

questions.text: The Mid-day Meal scheme was introduced to:
options: Increase enrollment and nutritional status of children
questions.explanation: The scheme encourages children to attend school and provides essential nutrition.

questions.text: Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) refers to the death of a child below the age of:
questions.explanation: IMR measures the number of deaths of infants under one year of age per 1,000 live births.

questions.text: Unemployment is said to exist when people who are willing to work at the prevailing wages:
questions.explanation: Unemployment describes the situation of people who are part of the workforce but cannot find employment.

questions.text: The 'workforce population' in India includes people from:
questions.explanation: Economically, the productive age group is considered to be 15 to 59 years.

questions.text: Which type of unemployment is common in urban areas?
questions.explanation: Many youth with degrees are unable to find suitable jobs in cities.

questions.text: When people are not able to find jobs during some months of the year, it is called:
questions.explanation: Seasonal unemployment is primarily found in the agricultural sector where work depends on crop cycles.

questions.text: In the case of disguised unemployment:
options: People look unemployed but are actually working
People appear to be employed but their contribution is zero
Work is available but people are lazy
questions.explanation: Disguised unemployment occurs when more people are engaged in an activity than are actually required.

questions.text: What is the consequence of unemployment?
questions.explanation: Unemployment has a detrimental impact on the overall growth of an economy.

questions.text: Which sector is the most labour-absorbing sector of the Indian economy?
questions.explanation: Agriculture continues to employ more than half of India's population.

questions.text: The story of Sakal shows that education leads to:
questions.explanation: Sakal's education helped him get a job and contribute to the growth of society.

questions.text: Why is the mortality rate declining in India?
questions.explanation: Widespread health infrastructure has led to increased life expectancy and lower death rates.

questions.text: Human capital formation adds to the ______ of the country.
questions.explanation: Human capital contributes directly to the national income and productivity.

questions.text: Which country invested heavily in human resources despite lacking natural resources?
questions.explanation: Japan became a developed country by investing in the education and health of its people.

questions.text: An educated parent is likely to invest more in their child's education because:
options: They realize the importance of education for growth
questions.explanation: They create a 'virtuous cycle' of investment in human capital.

questions.text: Birth rate is the number of babies born for every ________ people during a particular period.
questions.explanation: It is measured per 1000 individuals in the population.

questions.text: Disguised unemployment is mostly found in:
questions.explanation: It is common in family-owned farms where all family members work, even if not needed.

questions.text: Increase in life expectancy is an indicator of:
options: Good quality of life and health
questions.explanation: Living longer generally reflects better health and nutrition.

questions.text: Which sector has seen the maximum growth in employment in recent years in India?
questions.explanation: The service sector, including IT and biotechnology, is expanding rapidly.

questions.text: Vocational education is introduced to:
questions.explanation: It aims to make education more practical and job-oriented.

questions.text: GDP stands for:
questions.explanation: GDP is the total value of all goods and services produced within a country's borders.

questions.text: Literacy rate among males is ________ than among females in India.
questions.explanation: Due to historical and social factors, there remains a gender gap in literacy rates.

questions.text: Death rate is the number of people per 1000 who die during a:
questions.explanation: Standard demographic rates are usually calculated on an annual basis.

questions.text: Establishment of Navodaya Vidyalaya is for:
questions.explanation: These are residential schools aimed at providing high-quality education to rural talent.

questions.text: Investment in human capital is similar to investment in:
questions.explanation: Both require an initial cost to gain long-term productive benefits.

questions.text: What does GNP stand for?
questions.explanation: GNP is the total value of goods and services produced by the residents of a country.

questions.text: Economic activities that involve remuneration (pay) to anyone who performs it are:
questions.explanation: Market activities are done for profit or salary.

questions.text: Health is not only absence of disease but also:
questions.explanation: The efficiency of a person depends on their overall physical and mental well-being.

questions.text: Which of the following is a tertiary sector activity?
questions.explanation: Banking provides a service to both producers and consumers.

questions.text: Seasonal unemployment is primarily a problem of:
questions.explanation: In agriculture, workers are often busy only during sowing and harvesting.
